timeline.label: Q1 2026: net sales $181.5B (+17% YoY); AWS +28%; TTM OCF $148.5B vs productive-asset capex $151.0B
FY2025 10-K: net sales $716.9B (+12%); operating income $80.0B; net income $77.7B
Jeff Bezos Form 4 sales cluster (~909k shares); insider context only
AI infrastructure capex cycle: TTM capex approaches OCF; FCF temporarily collapsed
Andy Jassy CEO (since 2021); capital allocation focused on AWS AI and logistics density
Post-pandemic logistics right-sizing; retail margin recovery begins
20-for-1 stock split (June 2022); continued zero dividend policy
Andy Jassy succeeds Jeff Bezos as CEO; Bezos executive chair
Whole Foods acquisition closes; grocery footprint expansion
AWS resegmented as primary profit engine; retail reinvestment phase
AWS launched (S3, EC2); platform pivot beyond retail
IPO at $18/share (split-adjusted); long-term reinvestment doctrine in 1997 shareholder letter

industry.structure: Global e-commerce and cloud are oligopolistic at scale: Amazon, Microsoft, and Google dominate hyperscale cloud; retail competes with Walmart, Shopify ecosystem, and regional players. Logistics density and Prime create consumer lock-in.
industry.share_shift: AWS re-accelerated (+28% Q1 2026) while retail unit growth hit 15% (highest since COVID tail). Custom silicon (Trainium/Graviton) and advertising (>$70B TTM) gain mix share within Amazon.
industry.trend: AI workload capex arms race compresses reported free cash flow even as operating cash flow stays above $140B TTM; regulatory scrutiny on retail and cloud persists in US and EU.
